segments.text: Tonight, in a Channel 4 News special, the seven main parties go head-to-head on two of the biggest issues this election, immigration and law and order.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: Good evening from Colchester, the original capital of Roman Britain, a city established by some of the first people to come here in small boats across the Channel, the perfect location then to debate immigration.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: We're at the first site art centre in the shadow of Colchester Castle, used for centuries as a jail.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: It was famously grim, damp, run down and overcrowded.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: Sound familiar?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: For our first part, we're going to focus on law and order.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: We asked people across the UK, which of the following parties do you think would have the best policies for reducing crime?
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: Labour polled the best here on 35%, while 20% said the Conservatives, which is broadly in line with national voting intentions.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07

segments.text: 12% said reform, 4% the Lib Dems, 23% said they don't know.
segments.speaker: SPEAKER_07
